key_insight: A mesh-preserving self-attention surrogate can emulate PFEM free-surface fluid dynamics on evolving geometries while staying scalable.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster engineering simulation of complex free-surface and non-Newtonian flows without sacrificing the ability to reconstruct finite-element quantities like stress.
method: Uses a PFEM mesh as the model’s geometric/topological backbone.
Applies self-attention to capture interactions between mesh nodes over time.
Includes a linear attention variant to reduce cost and improve scalability.
evidence_signal: The models were evaluated on 2D and 3D free-surface benchmarks with changing geometry and material parameters, and they accurately reproduced transient dynamics and final states.
watch_item: As with any surrogate for evolving meshes, robustness during long rollouts and remeshing quality remain important implementation concerns.
paper_title: Attention mechanism for scalable mesh-based neural surrogates of free-surface fluids
